opinion.h: GROCHOWIAK v. PENNSYLVANIA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPERIOR COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA, PHILADELPHIA DISTRICT. No. 166. Decided October 13, 1958.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
John W. Keller and Mitchell Salem Fisher for appellant.
Thomas D. McBride, Attorney General of Pennsylvania, Frank P. Lawley, Jr. and Harry J. Rubin, Deputy Attorneys General, and George C. Eppinger for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS is of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
opinion.h: MARANZE v. MONTGOMERY COUNTY BOARD OF ELECTIONS, 358 U.S. 47 (1958)
opinion.p: 358 U.S. 47 MARANZE v. MONTGOMERY COUNTY BOARD OF ELECTIONS ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 144, Misc. Decided October 13, 1958.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
